[[File:Abyssal bludgeon detail.png|left|150px]]
The '''abyssal bludgeon''' is a two-handed [[crush weapon]] created by having [[The Overseer]] combine three untradeable components — the [[bludgeon axon]], [[bludgeon claw]], and [[bludgeon spine]]. If the player already had the Overseer create one, they can use the three components on the [[Overseer's book|book]] it left behind. Level 70 [[Attack]] and [[Strength]] are required to equip this weapon.

The components can be obtained from placing an [[unsired]], dropped by the [[Abyssal Sire]], in the [[Font of Consumption]]. The mean number of Sire kills to complete a bludgeon is 619.4, and the median number of kills is 552.

The bludgeon has a unique characteristic in that all of its attack styles are [[Combat Options#Aggressive|aggressive]], meaning it is only able to be used for [[Strength]] training. In addition, all of its attack styles use [[crush]].

==Special attack==
[[File:Penance.gif|frame|A player performs the abyssal bludgeon's special attack, ''Penance''.]]
The abyssal bludgeon has a [[special attacks|special attack]], ''Penance'', which increases damage dealt by 0.5% for every [[prayer point]] that the wielder is missing, consuming 50% of the wielder's special attack energy.

==Uses==
The abyssal bludgeon has the same attack speed as the [[abyssal whip]], and although it does not match the offensive bonuses of a whip combined with a [[dragon defender]] or [[avernic defender]], the bludgeon has the second highest overall damage of any crush weapon after the [[inquisitor's mace]], making it a good option against monsters weak to crush, such as [[gargoyle]]s or [[kalphites]].

The bludgeon is considered to be the fifth best weapon to train [[Strength]] with, behind the more expensive [[Ghrazi rapier]], [[blade of Saeldor]], [[Voidwaker]] and [[inquisitor's mace]]. Its damage per second is superior to every other non-degradable weapon to feature an "aggressive" attack style, making it a common weapon choice for mid-wealth players. [[Saradomin's blessed sword]] does possess a slightly higher Strength bonus than the bludgeon, but it is not cost-effective to use as it consumes [[Saradomin sword]]s.

The abyssal bludgeon, like the other abyssal weapons, is unaffected by the [[Tormented Demon]]'s fire shield, which reduces damage taken by 20%.

==Changes==
{{Subject changes header}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 19 March 2025
|update = Clan Events Improvements
|change = The [[special attack]] of the Abyssal bludgeon can once again give its damage increase effect in [[PvM]].
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 9 July 2020
|update = Grotesque Guardian Changes and Monkey Backpacks
|change = The sound effect when attacking with the abyssal bludgeon has been changed from [[:File:Unarmed attack (punch).wav|unarmed punch]] to [[bone club]]'s [[:File:Bone club attack.wav|attacking]] sound.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 11 January 2018
|update = The Return of Galvek
|change = The abyssal bludgeon's weight has been increased from 0 kg to 6 kg to roughly match the weights of the components required to create it (each being 2.5 kg).
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 22 October 2015
|update = Abyssal Bludgeon Buff
|poll = Abyssal buff
|change = The abyssal bludgeon's special attack now does 0.5% damage per missing prayer point instead of 0.3%.
}}
{{Subject changes footer}}

==Trivia==
*The process to create the weapon is similar to the [[noxious halberd]], which is in turn inspired by ''RuneScape 3''{{'}}s [[Rsw:spider leg|Spider leg]]; both items require the player to obtain three untradeable pieces in order to create said item.
*The bludgeon itself is 1.5kg lighter than the three individual components used to create it.
*The bludgeon reuses [[bone club]]'s attack and the [[granite maul]]'s ''Quick Smash'' special attack sound effects.
